scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Vrdhavastha Pension Yojana
page_title: Indira Gandhi National Vrdhavastha Pension Yojana

details: Indira Gandhi National Vridhavastha Pension Yojana, Under the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment Government of Rajasthan, provides financial support to the citizens whose families live below the poverty line. To empower senior citizens by ensuring their financial independence and dignity in old age, thereby enabling them to lead a dignified life.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
The age of the applicant (male or female) is 60 years or more.
The applicant should belong to a family living below the poverty line as per the criteria set by the Government of India.

online: Then you will be redirected to the SSO registration page. The registration page will appear with the following options.
Choose the either one option from the Jan Aadhaar Or Google to process further.
Jan Aadhaar : Enter the Jan Aadhaar number, click on the ‘Next’ button,
Select your name, the name of the head of the family and all the other members and
Click on the ‘Send OTP’ button. Enter the ‘OTP’ and
Click on the ‘Verify OTP’ button to Complete the registration.
Google : Enter the Gmail ID, click on the ‘Next’ button,
A new link appear on screen, now click on new SSO link.
SSO id will appear on screen, now create the password.
Enter Mobile number, click on registration.
Now click on "Apply for Scheme" on screen.
In this new page, will get a list of names of all the schemes,and click on the name of "Yojana"
Application form is free for Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme.

documents_required: Bank passbook front page copy (Account holder name, Account No, IFSC code).

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: Under this scheme, the Rajasthan government provides financial support to the citizen's families living below the poverty line.

faqs.question: What are the benefits of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The government gives a Pension amount: of Rs. 1000/- per month.

faqs.question: What is the age eligibility ?
faqs.answer: The age of the applicant (male or female) is 60 years or more.

faqs.question: What is B.P.L. card ?
faqs.answer: BPL (Below Poverty Line) ration card - This card was issued to households living below the poverty line.

faqs.question: Is this scheme benefit only for Rajasthan State?
faqs.answer: Yes, Applicants should be a native of Rajasthan State.

faqs.question: How do I know if a field in the application form is mandatory?
faqs.answer: There may be instructions or labels near the beginning of the form indicating that all fields marked with an asterisk (*) are mandatory.

faqs.question: What is Bhamashah card ?
faqs.answer: The Bhamashah card provided by this yojana is linked to a bank account in the name of the housewife. The card also provides women with biometric identification and core banking functions, as well as a variety of cash benefits that are directly transferred to their bank account.

faqs.question: Is this scheme for Urban area?
